water.name: Santa Ynez River and tributaries upstream of Gibraltar Dam

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(132) — Santa Ynez River and tributaries upstream of Gibraltar Dam (Santa Barbara Co.)
sources.quotedText: (132) Santa Ynez River and tributaries upstream of Gibraltar Dam (Santa Barbara Co.).  All year.  2 trout. 4 trout in possession.

sources.title: Gibraltar Reservoir | City of Santa Barbara
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 249134, "Santa Ynez River", Stream, CA, Santa Barbara Co.
sources.quotedText: GNIS has no dam-specific entry for Gibraltar Dam; location corroborated via OSM/Nominatim's "Gibraltar Dam" way (34.5265647,-119.6869630), cross-checked against the Wikipedia infobox coordinate for Gibraltar Dam (34.52694,-119.68694), and anchored to the Santa Ynez River's GNIS stream record (gaz_id 249134); correcting the file's prior coordinate (-119.65,34.55), which sat ~4.2km from the true dam location.

parameters.note: Covers the Santa Ynez River and its tributaries upstream of Gibraltar Dam, Santa Barbara Co. — the isolated headwater reach cut off from the anadromous lower river by Gibraltar and Bradbury dams.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit, 4 trout in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag limit, 4 trout in possession

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
